title: What did you expect going into the show
display_title: What did you expect going into the show
wikitext:
[]: What did you expect going into the show?             Quote Post by fukuda » Fri May 04, 2018 7:48 pm Who else expected Evangelion to be something dealing with gnosticism and theology? I could say that the idea of "Biblical Interpretation of Mechs" attracted me and the hope of Ascended Esoteric Knowledge?

[]: Re: Who else...             Quote Post by ACGT-Samael » Fri May 04, 2018 9:54 pm Kinda hilarious you'd post this now given I just made this thread. #shamelessselfpromotion All in all I like the Magitech approach Evangelion took with the stuff, even preferring it over the character psychology in some ways. Rebuild seems to lean more heavily on mysticism though.

[]: Re: Who else...             Quote Post by fukuda » Sat May 05, 2018 8:47 pm ACGT-Samael wrote: Kinda hilarious you'd post this now given I just made this thread. #shamelessselfpromotion All in all I like the Magitech approach Evangelion took with the stuff, even preferring it over the character psychology in some ways. Rebuild seems to lean more heavily on mysticism though.

[]: Re: Who else...             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun May 06, 2018 3:21 am fukuda wrote: Too bad the Rebuilds went with the name "Rebuild"

[]: Re: Who else...             Quote Post by Joseki » Sun May 06, 2018 8:10 am Isn't "Rebuild" the name of the project as a whole? I remember seeing pictures of the staff with a "Rebuild" t-shirt.

[]: Re: Who else...             Quote Post by ACGT-Samael » Sun May 06, 2018 8:13 am Joseki wrote: Isn't "Rebuild" the name of the project as a whole? I remember seeing pictures of the staff with a "Rebuild" t-shirt.

[]: Re: Who else...             Quote Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Sun May 06, 2018 10:51 am fukuda wrote: Who else expected Evangelion to be something dealing with gnosticism and theology? I could say that the idea of "Biblical Interpretation of Mechs" attracted me and the hope of Ascended Esoteric Knowledge?

[]: Re: Who else...             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Mon May 07, 2018 11:18 am I remember always thinking it would be this edgy, nihilistic, pessimistic, hard to understand jumble of depression that shits all over it's fans, after all, that's how most people see it, how it's portrayed by people online. I vividly remember ironically picking up the first volume of the Sadamoto's manga adaption in the high school library and instantly being blown away by the art and realistic characters and their interactions. I made the quick decision after the first chapter to watch the series when I got home. I identified so strongly with Shinji that I binged the entire series, End of Evangelion, and all three New Theatrical films in a week, I was fuckin hooked. God, I nearly vomited when I learned that that shot of Shinji looking at his hand during the opening sequence of AIR was a real thing and not doctored. I specifically remember closing my Chromebook to get up and walk around to try to process what the ever living fuck I just watched after both AIR and I Need You, and I remember checking over and over to make sure I was watching the right movie while streaming Q, making sure I didn't skip anything between it and Ha.

[]: Re: Who else...             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue May 08, 2018 7:11 am Just for the future, thread titles should be descriptive and give you a good idea of the topic and contents. The original thread title, "Who else...", leaves a bit to be desired on this front, and is basically clickbait, so I'm fixing it. Re: "Rebuild", my best understanding is that it was a provisional project title that was ultimately relegated to a buzz word for the process of creating the films.

[]: Re: Who else...             Quote Post by ACGT-Samael » Tue May 08, 2018 1:27 pm Reichu wrote: Re: "Rebuild", my best understanding is that it was a provisional project title that was ultimately relegated to a buzz word for the process of creating the films.

[]: Re: What did you expect going into the show?             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu May 24, 2018 3:42 pm Sorta knew what I was getting into, didn't expect it to be so raw and real though.

[]: Re: What did you expect going into the show?             Quote Post by The Cruel » Sat May 26, 2018 2:13 am Nothing. I was barely stumbling over Eva a fiew times while reading doujins, saw some reviews without to spoil myself, heard it's one of the most importaint animes in history and when I did watched it, there was no turning back and since then for five years, Eva is a importaint part of my life now.

[]: Re: What did you expect going into the show?             Quote Post by barnett » Wed May 30, 2018 6:50 am That's the best way. I discovered in TV years ago (starting from the second episode), and at the time I did not have internet at home. No spoilers, no reviews, no binge watching: an unadulterated experience. What a rollercoaster it was!
